docs.title: Data Science Clear Insights • Reproducible ML • Practical AI Agents
docs.text:
I’m an early‑stage Data Science and Machine Learning practitioner focused on turning data into meaningful insights and dependable tools. I work with structured analysis, reproducible workflows, and modern AI assistants to build solutions that support real decision‑making.

This portfolio presents selected projects that reflect how I think and how I work. Each one highlights clarity, reliability, and user‑oriented design.

docs.title: Featured Projects
docs.text:
• Exploratory Data Analysis of the Iris dataset, including a structured workflow, visual insights, and a polished article.

  • Half‑Marathon Predictor App, a complete ML application built with a reproducible training pipeline and an interactive interface.

These examples show my approach to practical machine learning: clean structure, transparent reasoning, and tools that are easy to understand and use.

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
Hi, I’m Marek, an early‑stage Data Science and Machine Learning practitioner focused on building clear, reliable, and practical solutions. My work combines structured analysis, reproducible workflows, and user‑oriented design to create tools that genuinely help people make better decisions.

I enjoy turning complex problems into understandable, actionable insights. Whether I’m exploring a dataset, designing a model, or building an interactive app, I aim for clarity, transparency, and long‑term maintainability.

docs.title: How I Work
docs.text:
I design reproducible ML workflows with clean separation between data preparation, model training, and deployment.

I focus on clear insights, making sure that results are understandable and meaningful for real users.

I build practical tools, from exploratory analyses to full applications, always with usability in mind.

I value collaboration, structured communication, and thoughtful documentation.

I work collaboratively with AI tools such as GitHub Copilot. I don’t create every line of code myself, but I understand, review, and correct the generated code, and I use it to build clear, reproducible, and practical solutions.

docs.title: What I’m Interested In
docs.text:
• Machine learning that solves real problems

  • Interactive applications that make data accessible

  • Clean, maintainable code and transparent workflows

  • AI agents and modern automation tools

  • Visual clarity and user‑centered design

docs.title: Beyond the Code
docs.text:
I enjoy learning, experimenting, and refining my workflow. I treat each project as an opportunity to improve both the technical and the human side of data science: communication, structure, and design.

If you’d like to collaborate or discuss an idea, feel free to reach out through GitHub.

docs.title: Half‑Marathon Predictor App
docs.text: This project delivers a practical machine learning application that predicts half‑marathon finish times based on a runner’s training history and personal characteristics. It demonstrates a complete, reproducible workflow: data preparation, model training, evaluation, and deployment as an interactive web app.

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text:
The goal of this project is to build a reliable and user‑friendly tool that helps runners estimate their expected half‑marathon performance. The app is designed for both hobby runners and coaches who want quick, data‑driven predictions.

The workflow includes:

  • structured data preprocessing
  • feature engineering based on training metrics
  • model selection and evaluation
  • error analysis and bias checks
  • deployment as a Streamlit application

docs.title: How the Model Works
docs.text:
The model predicts finish time using features such as:

  • recent training volume
  • pace distribution
  • long‑run performance
  • personal characteristics

The final model was selected based on performance, stability, and interpretability. The training pipeline is fully reproducible and separated from the app code.

docs.title: The App
docs.text:
The Streamlit app allows users to:

  • input their training data
  • adjust key parameters
  • receive an estimated finish time
  • view model confidence and interpretation

The interface is clean, minimalistic, and optimized for clarity.

docs.title: Why This Project Matters
docs.text:
This project shows how machine learning can be turned into a practical tool that supports real‑world decision‑making. It highlights your focus on:

  • reproducible ML
  • user‑oriented design
  • clear insights
  • reliable deployment

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The HM Predictor App follows a clean, reproducible structure that separates data preparation, model training, and application logic.

User Input ↓ Streamlit App (UI Layer) ↓ Feature Processing ↓ Trained ML Model (loaded from artifacts) ↓ Prediction Engine ↓ Result Display (finish time + interpretation)

• Iris Dataset Project
    • Spis treści
    • Contents
    • Overview

This project explores the classic Iris dataset through a clean, reproducible workflow. It includes an exploratory data analysis, a polished article, and the original notebook.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The goal of this project is to demonstrate clear insights, structured analysis, and a reproducible workflow. It covers:

  • data exploration and visualization
  • feature relationships
  • class separability
  • interpretation of patterns
